## SCENE 3: The Recognition (395 CE, Hippo, North Africa)

Augustine of Hippo was sixty-one when he finally understood what he'd been teaching
- Enough that everyone watching understood: the Empire was finished
- When Generation 112 inherits all the fragments—genetic memory stronger than ours, bronze keys rediscovered, network reassembled—they'll have what they need

- Not the Greek he'd studied in school, but a dialect from four hundred years ago
- Later, at the library, he'd found the exact phrases in a manuscript of Origen's *De Principiis*
